title: 控方证人
summary:
Following his conversation with Bernard Seldon regarding the struggle between his physical desires and spiritual visions, Sylas Hammel resolves to seek out the "cripple" Seldon had suggested. The next morning, Hammel finds the man in a dark alley, where the performer is using colored chalk to draw lifelike landscapes on the pavement. When an agitated Hammel demands to know the man's identity, the performer draws a figure with a beautiful face and goat legs playing a tubular instrument. The man warns Hammel that "they are evil," and Hammel becomes mesmerized by the drawing, identifying the face as that of Pan.

This encounter triggers a profound internal crisis. While sitting in a park, Hammel realizes that his attachment to wealth is the chain binding him and preventing him from answering the "call of wings" that demands total freedom. To achieve this liberation, he decides to sacrifice his most precious possession. Hammel travels to the East End of London to meet Boris (also known as Bolo), a man he considers trustworthy. He informs Boris that he has spent two weeks finalizing legal documents to donate his entire fortune to the poor of East London, appointing Boris as the trustee.

After leaving Boris, Hammel experiences a sudden, painful sense of fear regarding poverty, hunger, and cold, yet he feels the pressure of his former life has been removed. Possessing only two pennies, he decides to take the metro to the park. As he descends the stairs to the metro platform, he senses an evil presence in the heavy air and notices a man sitting alone on a chair, appearing to be either drunk or unconscious.
